note: Every invalid fixture is a well-formed document with exactly one defect planted in it, and declares the single finding it must produce. Asserting the code AND the path (not merely 'it was rejected') is what keeps a rule from silently going dead: a fixture that is rejected for the wrong reason still looks green.

valid.covers: A green Required graph. Also pins that an advisory skip carrying a machine-readable reason, with a null end time, is legitimate and does not gate.
An honestly red proof. A required-proof recording a failure is valid evidence; only a verdict the records do not support is not.
A validated candidate. Also pins that a failed advisory job and a still-running advisory job do not block a release.
A scoped mutation run whose declared rate recomputes, every survivor is classified, and every claimed kill carries both witnesses.
A close backed by a required-proof at the same SHA, with limitations stated rather than implied.

invalid.covers: An abbreviated SHA. Evidence must name an exact commit; 4f2a9c1e is a prefix, and prefixes collide.
A required command skipped, with the proof still declaring pass. The skip even carries an honest reason: it is the promotion to pass that is the lie.
A command claiming outcome pass that never recorded an end. It cannot have passed; it never finished.
A completed pass with no process status. A pass/fail record must name the exit status that produced its outcome.
A required command claiming pass after exit status 1. A forged outcome cannot turn a failed process into green evidence.
A required command claiming failure after exit status 0. Outcome and status must agree in both directions.
A v2 proof whose records omit a command named by its committed Required graph. A pass over a subset is not evidence of the full graph.
Python accepts bare NaN while decoding JSON, but evidence rates must be finite before numeric bounds or arithmetic can be trusted.
A declared kill rate of 0.9 over counts that yield 0.75. Unreachable by JSON Schema, which has no arithmetic: this is why the contract has a semantic layer at all.
A version-matrix artifact recorded for the parent commit, offered as evidence for this one. The parent-artifact substitution.
A required job still in progress. Non-terminal is not success; the subtler half of 'never call CI green while a required job is non-success'.
A v0.8.4 tag on a tree that ships 0.8.3.
A passing test result carried over from the parent commit into this proof.
A proof produced from uncommitted changes, describing code that exists at no commit.
A mutation run whose second shard never finished, making its counts a floor rather than a measurement.
Three kills claimed, two witnessed. The third is an assertion.
A mutation run without a completion instant cannot turn partial counts into a measurement.
Every survivor must carry a taxonomy, not merely contribute to counts.missed.
One witnessed kill repeated twice must not inflate counts.caught.
One classified survivor repeated twice must not inflate counts.missed.
A mutant cannot be claimed as both killed and surviving.
The rule the bead asked to be documented, executed instead: a scoped test cannot mark a bead ready.
A close claiming live verification with nothing to point at.
A defect known at close time with no bead tracking it: how a known defect becomes an unknown one.
